description: Dashboard MOTD: attributed quotations from widely published sources (many are historical / public-domain). A random line is chosen once per page load when the on-device LLM is unavailable. Verify licensing for your jurisdiction before redistribution.
messages: Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much. — Keller
Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement. — Keller
Nothing can be done without hope and confidence. — Keller
Keep your face to the sunshine and you cannot see a shadow. — Keller
Character cannot be developed in ease and quiet. — Keller
Walking with a friend in the dark is better than walking alone in the light. — Keller
The best and most beautiful things must be felt with the heart. — Keller
Although the world is full of suffering, it is full of the overcoming of it. — Keller
Science may have set limits, but no limits have been set on imagination. — Keller
The only thing worse than being blind is having sight but no vision. — Keller
The highest result of education is tolerance. — Keller
We could never learn to be brave if there were only joy in the world. — Keller
The only thing we have to fear is fear itself. — F. Roosevelt
Do what you can, with what you have, where you are. — T. Roosevelt
Believe you can and you are halfway there. — T. Roosevelt
When you reach the end of your rope, tie a knot and hang on. — F. Roosevelt
You must do the thing you think you cannot do. — E. Roosevelt
The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams. — E. Roosevelt
With the new day comes new strength and new thoughts. — E. Roosevelt
No one can make you feel inferior without your consent. — E. Roosevelt
I walk slowly, but I never walk backward. — Lincoln
I am not bound to win, but I am bound to be true. — Lincoln
Most folks are as happy as they make up their minds to be. — Lincoln
I will prepare and someday my chance will come. — Lincoln
If there is no struggle, there is no progress. — Douglass
It is easier to build strong children than to repair broken men. — Douglass
The soul that is within me no man can degrade. — Douglass
Power concedes nothing without a demand. — Douglass
Every great dream begins with a dreamer. — Tubman
Never ran my train off the track, and I never lost a passenger. — Tubman
I think slavery is the next thing to hell. — Tubman
Truth is powerful and it prevails. — Truth
I am not going to die, I am going home like a shooting star. — Truth
Independence is happiness. — Anthony
Failure is impossible. — Anthony
Men their rights, and nothing more; women their rights, and nothing less. — Anthony
To be yourself in a world that would change you is life’s greatest accomplishment. — Emerson
Go where there is no path and leave a trail. — Emerson
To be great is to be misunderstood. — Emerson
It is not the length of life, but the depth of life. — Emerson
Finish each day and be done with it; you have done what you could. — Emerson
Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm. — Emerson
What lies within us is greater than what lies before or behind us. — Emerson
The purpose of life is not to be happy. It is to be useful, to be honourable. — Emerson
Dare to live the life you have dreamed for yourself. — Emerson
Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined. — Thoreau
The price of anything is the amount of life you exchange for it. — Thoreau
Things do not change; we change. — Thoreau
It is never too late to give up our prejudices. — Thoreau
Success usually comes to those who are too busy to be looking for it. — Thoreau
Heaven is under our feet as well as over our heads. — Thoreau
The mass of men lead lives of quiet desperation. — Thoreau
Keep your face always toward the sunshine—and shadows will fall behind you. — Whitman
Re-examine all you have been told; dismiss what insults your soul. — Whitman
I am large, I contain multitudes. — Whitman
Happiness, not in another place but this place—not for another hour, but this hour. — Whitman
Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul. — Dickinson
Forever is composed of nows. — Dickinson
If I can stop one heart from breaking, I shall not live in vain. — Dickinson
We turn not older with years, but newer every day. — Dickinson
The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Aurelius
You have power over your mind—not outside events. — Aurelius
Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one. — Aurelius
The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts. — Aurelius
When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive. — Aurelius
If it is not right, do not do it; if it is not true, do not say it. — Aurelius
No man is free who is not master of himself. — Epictetus
First say to yourself what you would be; then do what you have to do. — Epictetus
No great thing is created suddenly. — Epictetus
Difficulties are things that show a person what they are. — Epictetus
Sometimes even to live is an act of courage. — Seneca
Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labour does the body. — Seneca
Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life. — Seneca
Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity. — Seneca
It is the power of the mind to be unconquerable. — Seneca
Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall. — Confucius
It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop. — Confucius
The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones. — Confucius
I hear and I forget. I see and I remember. I do and I understand. — Confucius
The secret of getting ahead is getting started. — Twain
Courage is resistance to fear, mastery of fear—not absence of fear. — Twain
Be yourself; everyone else is already taken. — Wilde
We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars. — Wilde
To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all. — Wilde
It was the best of times, it was the worst of times. — Dickens
No one is useless in this world who lightens the burdens of another. — Dickens
Reflect upon your present blessings—not on your past misfortunes. — Dickens
There is nothing in the world so irresistibly contagious as laughter. — Dickens
I am the master of my fate: I am the captain of my soul. — Henley
Out of the night that covers me, black as the pit from pole to pole. — Henley
Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise. — Hugo
He who opens a school door, closes a prison. — Hugo
It is never too late to be what you might have been. — Eliot
Only in the agony of parting do we look into the depths of love. — Eliot
There is no charm equal to tenderness of heart. — Austen
Know your own happiness. Want nothing but patience—or call it hope. — Austen
I am no bird; and no net ensnares me: I am a free human being. — Brontë
Life appears to me too short to be spent in nursing animosity. — Brontë
Tomorrow is a new day with no mistakes in it yet. — Montgomery
Nothing is worth more than this day. — Goethe
Whatever you can do or dream you can, begin it; boldness has genius in it. — Goethe
Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do. — Goethe
To thine own self be true. — Shakespeare
We know what we are, but know not what we may be. — Shakespeare
Our doubts are traitors, and make us lose the good we oft might win. — Shakespeare
Sweet are the uses of adversity. — Shakespeare
The miserable have no other medicine but only hope. — Shakespeare
Come what come may, time and the hour runs through the roughest day. — Shakespeare
The night is long that never finds the day. — Shakespeare
Things won are done; joy’s soul lies in the doing. — Shakespeare
Brevity is the soul of wit. — Shakespeare
Cowards die many times; the valiant taste of death but once. — Shakespeare
The robbed that smiles steals something from the thief. — Shakespeare
They also serve who only stand and wait. — Milton
What though the field be lost? All is not lost. — Milton
A mind not to be changed by place or time. — Milton
Long is the way and hard, that out of Hell leads up to light. — Milton
The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell. — Milton
I will not cease from mental fight, nor shall my sword sleep in my hand. — Blake
He who binds to himself a joy does the winged life destroy. — Blake
Energy is eternal delight. — Blake
If the fool would persist in his folly he would become wise. — Blake
Tyger Tyger, burning bright, in the forests of the night. — Blake
Beauty in things exists in the mind which contemplates them. — Hume
A little learning is a dangerous thing. — Pope
Fools rush in where angels fear to tread. — Pope
Hope springs eternal in the human breast. — Pope
The world is full of things—we should all be as happy as kings. — Stevenson
Don’t judge each day by the harvest you reap but by the seeds that you plant. — Stevenson
Judge yourself not by your harvest, but by your seeds. — Stevenson
To travel hopefully is a better thing than to arrive. — Stevenson
A friend is a present you give yourself. — Stevenson
Hitch your wagon to a star. — Emerson
Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string. — Emerson
Write it on your heart that every day is the best day in the year. — Emerson
Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience. — Emerson
The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn. — Emerson
Make the most of yourself, for that is all there is of you. — Emerson
In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks. — Muir
The mountains are calling and I must go. — Muir
Character, not circumstances, makes the person. — B. T. Washington
The heights by great men reached were not attained by sudden flight. — Longfellow
Look not mournfully into the past. It comes not back again. — Longfellow
Well done is better than well said. — Franklin
